interventions.name: Rapamycin (Sirolimus)

mechanism.pathway: Nutrient sensing / deregulated nutrient sensing hallmark

studies.result: 14% median lifespan extension when started at 20 months

studies.citation: Miller RA et al. J Gerontol A. 2014;69(2):119-130
studies.result: Replicated across multiple sites, both sexes

mouse.notes: Most robust pharmacological intervention in ITP program

trials.name: PEARL Trial (Participatory Evaluation of Aging with Rapamycin)

human.notes: No completed longevity trials; diabetes/transplant data not applicable

concerns: Increased infection risk at high doses
safety.long_term_data: Decades of transplant use; longevity dosing (<5mg/week) safety unclear

practical.dosing: Experimental longevity protocols: 3-8mg weekly (vs 2-5mg daily for transplant)

interventions.translation_notes: Lifespan extension consistent in mice; human applicability unknown. Immunosuppression may offset benefits in healthy humans.
interventions.recommendation: Insufficient evidence for general use. Consider only with medical supervision and biomarker monitoring.

interventions.name: Metformin

mechanism.primary: Mitochondrial complex I inhibition → AMPK activation

studies.result: No significant lifespan extension in most strains

studies.citation: Martin-Montalvo A et al. Nat Commun. 2013;4:2192

mouse.notes: Benefits in diabetes models may not translate to healthy aging

human.status: TAME trial (Targeting Aging with Metformin) enrolling
human.observational: Diabetic patients on metformin show reduced mortality vs other antidiabetics (confounded)
human.notes: Diabetes benefit ≠ longevity benefit in healthy individuals

safety.long_term_data: Extensive safety data in diabetes; longevity use profile similar

practical.accessibility: Prescription required; widely prescribed for diabetes

interventions.translation_notes: Weak animal evidence despite strong mechanistic rationale. Human trial results awaited.
interventions.recommendation: Promising but unproven. TAME trial results needed. Consider only with medical supervision.

interventions.name: NAD+ Precursors (NMN, NR)

mouse.effect_size: Modest metabolic improvements; minimal lifespan data

studies.citation: Mills KF et al. Cell Metab. 2016;24(6):795-806
studies.result: NMN improves glucose tolerance, lipid profile in aged mice

mouse.notes: Healthspan benefits; no rigorous lifespan studies in ITP

trials.citation: Yoshino M et al. npj Aging. 2021;7:9
trials.result: NMN increases NAD+ in muscle; no functional improvements detected

trials.citation: Remie CME et al. Am J Clin Nutr. 2020;112(2):413-426
trials.result: NR supplementation: no metabolic benefit in healthy adults

human.notes: Biomarker changes (NAD+ increase) without clinical benefits in most studies

practical.accessibility: Widely available as supplement (regulatory status uncertain)

interventions.translation_notes: Biomarker effects clear; functional/longevity benefits absent in human trials to date.
interventions.recommendation: Insufficient evidence for longevity benefits. Expensive intervention without demonstrated outcomes.

interventions.name: Senolytics (Dasatinib + Quercetin)

mouse.effect_size: +36% remaining lifespan when started late-life (20 months)

studies.result: D+Q extends healthspan and lifespan in naturally aged mice

studies.citation: Zhu Y et al. Aging Cell. 2015;14(4):644-658
studies.result: D+Q clears senescent cells in vitro and in vivo

mouse.notes: Most impressive effect when started late in life; targets existing damage

human.status: Phase I/II trials completed; larger trials ongoing

trials.result: D+Q safe in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis; functional improvements
D+Q reduces senescent cell burden in diabetic kidney disease

human.notes: Safety established; efficacy for aging vs. specific diseases being tested

safety.profile: Intermittent dosing (3 days/month) reduces side effects
concerns: Long-term effect of repeated senescent cell clearance unknown
safety.long_term_data: Intermittent dosing data emerging; continuous dasatinib not recommended

practical.dosing: Dasatinib 100mg + Quercetin 1000mg for 3 consecutive days per month
practical.monitoring: CBC before each cycle; bleeding risk assessment

interventions.translation_notes: Strong mechanistic rationale; human trials show senescent cell reduction but longevity endpoint TBD.
interventions.recommendation: Most promising senolytic. Consider only with medical supervision and appropriate monitoring.

interventions.name: Aerobic Exercise

mouse.effect_size: Variable; difficult to translate voluntary vs forced exercise
mouse.notes: Healthspan benefits clearer than lifespan extension in rodents

human.status: Strongest evidence of any longevity intervention

observational.result: 15 min/day exercise → 3 year life expectancy increase

observational.citation: Lee DC et al. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2014;64(5):472-481
observational.result: Running 5-10 min/day → 3 year life expectancy gain

observational.citation: Mandsager K et al. JAMA Netw Open. 2018;1(6):e183605
observational.result: Each 1-MET increase in VO2max → 10-25% mortality reduction

human.notes: Dose-response relationship; benefits continue beyond guidelines

concerns: Cardiac events in unscreened high-risk individuals
safety.long_term_data: Decades of data; benefits clear and consistent

practical.dosing: 150 min/week moderate intensity minimum; benefits increase to ~300 min/week
practical.intensity: Zone 2 (conversational pace) for base; some higher intensity beneficial

interventions.translation_notes: Direct human evidence. No translation needed.
interventions.recommendation: Single strongest longevity intervention. Universal recommendation barring contraindications.

interventions.name: Caloric Restriction

studies.citation: McCay CM et al. J Nutr. 1935;10(1):63-79
studies.result: Foundational study: CR extends rat lifespan
Consistent lifespan extension across strains and labs

mouse.notes: Most robust and replicated lifespan intervention in rodents

primate.effect_size: Inconsistent results between two major studies

studies.citation: Mattison JA et al. Nat Commun. 2017;8:14063
studies.result: NIA study: healthspan benefit, no lifespan extension

studies.citation: Colman RJ et al. Nat Commun. 2014;5:3557
studies.result: Wisconsin study: survival benefit, reduced age-related disease

primate.notes: Diet composition and study design differences may explain discrepancy

trials.citation: Kraus WE et al. J Gerontol A. 2019;74(1):162-165
trials.result: Metabolic benefits, reduced inflammation, no longevity endpoint

human.notes: Biomarker improvements; lifespan impact unknown and untestable in humans

safety.long_term_data: CALERIE provides human data; <25% restriction safer

practical.duration: Lifelong required for benefits in animal models
practical.adherence: Extremely difficult; compliance poor in free-living humans

interventions.translation_notes: Robust rodent data; primate data mixed; human lifespan untested. Severe restrictions may cause net harm.
interventions.recommendation: Not recommended for longevity in humans. Time-restricted eating or moderate restriction may provide benefits with better adherence.

interventions.name: Partial Cellular Reprogramming

mouse.effect_size: Reverses aging markers; extends lifespan in progeroid models

studies.result: OSK expression extends lifespan and ameliorates aging in progeria mice
In vivo OSK reprogramming restores vision in glaucoma model

studies.citation: Browder KC et al. Nat Aging. 2022;2:243-253
studies.result: Reprogramming improves muscle regeneration in aged mice

mouse.notes: Breakthrough approach; proof-of-concept strong in mice

human.trials: Altos Labs, Turn Bio, Life Biosciences developing clinical programs
human.notes: No human data yet; major safety validation required

concerns: Dosage and timing critical (too much = cancer, too little = no effect)
Long-term stability of reprogrammed state unclear

practical.delivery: Gene therapy vectors (AAV, lentivirus) or mRNA
practical.dosing: Pulsed expression (days to weeks, not continuous)
practical.cost: Not commercially available; gene therapy pricing TBD
practical.accessibility: Research only; years from clinical access

interventions.translation_notes: Revolutionary approach in mice. Human safety entirely unknown. Teratoma risk must be definitively excluded.
interventions.recommendation: Experimental only. Most promising long-term intervention but human trials needed. Monitor clinical trial progress.

interventions.name: Young Plasma / Parabiosis

mechanism.primary: Transfer of pro-youthful factors or dilution of pro-aging factors
secondary: Or: dilution of inflammatory cytokines, senescent cell factors

mouse.effect_size: Reverses aging markers in heterochronic parabiosis

studies.result: Young blood rejuvenates aged muscle and liver

studies.citation: Villeda SA et al. Nat Med. 2014;20(6):659-663
studies.result: Young blood reverses age-related cognitive impairment

studies.citation: Rebo J et al. Nat Commun. 2016;7:13363
studies.result: Plasma dilution (without young blood) also beneficial

mouse.notes: Mechanism unclear: add youth factors vs. remove aging factors

trials.result: No rigorous trial data; FDA warning issued

human.notes: Human trials failed to replicate mouse benefits

safety.profile: Plasma infusion risks standard for transfusion
concerns: Unknown long-term effects of repeated plasma exchange

practical.accessibility: Largely unavailable; most clinics shut down

interventions.translation_notes: Dramatic mouse results failed to translate to humans. Mechanism remains poorly understood.
interventions.recommendation: Not recommended. Human trials failed. Expensive intervention without demonstrated benefit. Possible harms.

evidence_levels.very_high: Multiple large RCTs or consistent cohort studies
evidence_levels.high: RCT data or large observational studies with strong effect
evidence_levels.moderate: Limited RCT or observational data with moderate effect
evidence_levels.low: Animal data only or very limited human data
evidence_levels.preliminary: Early-stage research or surrogate endpoints only
